_update_guide: Generated by generate_dashboard.py. Do NOT edit by hand. Update task frontmatter (C:\Bari\tasks\TASK-*.md), decisions.json, or category_config.json, then re-run: python generate_dashboard.py. Between runs, python check_drift.py refreshes drift alerts without a full regenerate.

next_action.title: F2 - protein-bar matrix discount + collagen marker in Protein Quality dimension

tasks.title: Comparison Platform v2 Completion (existing fleet)
tasks.summary: Drive the accepted v2 migration (TASK-098/118) across the 5 live gen1 categories to consumer- and publication-ready standard. Owns launch dimensions: categories on v2 + field-complete, platform capabilities (v2 Phase 1+2, hub, SEO). Prereq gate: author+sign comparison_ui_reference_v2.md; QA re-baseline. base_pct deferred to Phase 3 (does not gate launch). milk = 128E, committed post-launch (DEC-003 Amendment A).

tasks.title: Fix P0 confidence gate validation
tasks.summary: Confidence gate validates ingredient presence rather than ingredient quality; fix per hummus/maadanim/snacks/milk failure cases

tasks.title: Reconcile yogurts to a frozen machine run, then score-freeze
tasks.summary: Per TASK-129-A audit + Controller decision (2026-06-01): retire the v1-mvp-manual hand-tuned yogurt scores as source of truth. Regenerate from run_yogurt_001, diff vs the 13 displayed scores, resolve each delta, freeze the reconciled corpus. Unblocks yogurts frontend NO-GO (launch gate).

tasks.title: BSIP Calibration & Confidence (launch slice)
tasks.summary: Launch-gating scoring slice split from old O2: hummus confidence accuracy re-audit (gates v2 Phase-0 #6 in TASK-128), calibration of observed weaknesses, and per-category SCORE FREEZE before each launch category feeds articles/launch. BSIP2 next-gen evolution is deferred post-launch (separate, not this task). Owns launch dimension: BSIP maturity (confidence gate, score-freeze).

tasks.title: Freeze bread on retail_003 authority; close limitingFactors gap
tasks.summary: Per TASK-129-A audit + Controller decision (2026-06-01): declare bread_retail_003 the authoritative launch corpus, archive/relabel bread_light synthesis as experimental, then score-freeze the 24 displayed. Gated on frontend closing the bread limitingFactors field gap (Launch-Definition section 6 / TASK-128). Moves bread from BLOCKED to CONDITIONAL GO.

tasks.title: Operating-Model Hardening
tasks.summary: New workstream (specified by category_scaling_readiness_v1): validate-corpus command enforcing v2 field-completeness (incl. closing bread limitingFactors gap), self-contained category-module contract, route/status governance + deprecation policy, clean committed baseline. Gate for Wave 2 (TASK-132). Owns launch dimension: QA threshold (validate-corpus, baselines).

tasks.title: Implement approved BSIP2 Evidence-Watch 2026-06-01 revisions (F2, F1, F4)
tasks.summary: Objective: ship the owner-approved scoring revisions from the 2026-06-01 Evidence Watch evaluation (TASK-132) - F2 protein-bar matrix discount, F1 emulsifier identity tiering, F4 BHA named-additive penalty. F3 deferred. Sub-tasks A-D; taxonomy-first sequencing.

tasks.title: Named-additive + ingredient-fragmentation taxonomy (matrix Req 1) - enabling infra
tasks.summary: Build the ingredient taxonomy with fragmentation level + named-additive identity metadata that matrix_integrity_framework.md Req 1 specifies. Enabling infra for F2/F1/F4: lets the scorer distinguish ingredient FORM (oats vs oat isolate; carrageenan/CMC vs lecithin; native vs modified starch) and named additives. Build once; F2/F1/F4 all ride on it.

tasks.title: F2 - protein-bar matrix discount + collagen marker in Protein Quality dimension
tasks.summary: ADOPT (High). Discount the Protein Quality dimension contribution for bar-format + protein_isolate/reconstructed-source products (empirical DIAAS 47-81% of label; use as a conservative band, not per-product). Add collagen marker + extra discount (incomplete AA profile, lowest matrix DIAAS). Coordinate via PROCESSING_LOAD family to avoid double-count with NOVA-4/additive caps. Closes the documented protein-isolate-bar gaming hole.

tasks.title: F1 - identity-modulated emulsifier/stabilizer penalty weights
tasks.summary: ADAPT (Med-High). Replace flat emulsifier -6 with identity-modulated weights: carrageenan (E407)/CMC (E466) up; soy lecithin (E322) down toward neutral (corrects current over-penalty); native (unmodified) rice starch excluded from additive burden, modified starch unchanged. Single RCT n=60 -> keep deltas modest; note food-grade vs degraded carrageenan. No new caps (Tension-5 rule budget).

tasks.title: F4 - BHA named-additive penalty (differentiate BHT); verify FDA GRAS status first
tasks.summary: WATCH (Low). Cheap piggyback once the named-additive lookup exists: add BHA (E320) as a small named penalty in the existing additive/processing dimension; explicitly differentiate BHT (NOT under reassessment). Do NOT build regulatory-status tracking. Verify current FDA GRAS/reassessment outcome first - Apr-2026 comment window has closed and the spring-2026 GRAS rule may have landed.

tasks.summary: Author the binding comparison_ui_reference_v2.md engineering reference (Phase-1 prerequisite gate for TASK-128). tasks.title: Implement Registry First Rule across Agent OS
tasks.summary: Establish the Registry First Rule: any task-management request (status/close/accept/reject/block/resume/reopen of a TASK-XXX) must consult the registry before answering. tasks.title: Comparison UI Reference v2 implementation roadmap
tasks.summary: Implementation roadmap for Comparison UI Reference v2 (no implementation, no redesign). Four phases: Phase 0 v1-safe promotions (#6 confidence promote+gate, #8 disclosure de-dup, relativity) → Phase 1 v2 core (#1 density, #2 metric block protein+additives only, #5 rowReason, #7 expansion, #9 responsive table) → Phase 2 nav chrome (#3 band rail, #4 dividers, histogram) → Phase 3 base_pct completion (deferred on new main-ingredient extraction pipeline). base_pct removed from Phase 1 per TASK-111. VM deps, 5 downstream blockers, category migration order (hummus→maadanim→snacks→yogurts→bread→milk), pilot = hummus. Flagged: "TASK-106 decision" input not locatable; TASK-098/DEC-002 govern. Accepted and CLOSED by Central Controller 2026-05-31.

decisions.title: HUM-001 fat data strategy for Hummus v1
options: A — Display fat values despite corruption
B — Suppress fat display in Hummus v1
C — Delay launch until fat values corrected
decisions.decision: Option B — Suppress fat_g and saturated_fat_g from hummus frontend JSON. Add disclosure sentence to MethodologyFooter. v2 fat correction scheduled Q3 2026.
decisions.notes: See TASK-060 for full evaluation and rationale.

decisions.title: Hummus v1 go-live approval
options: Hold until QA warning W-1 is resolved
decisions.recommendation: Hold until TASK-073 resolves QA warning W-1 (content spec update), then approve.
decisions.decision: Approve go-live. W-1 resolved and final QA passed (TASK-090); Hummus v1 is LIVE.
decisions.notes: Gated on TASK-073. W-1 cleared and final QA passed (TASK-090); go-live approved (TASK-096).

decisions.title: Bari Launch Definition v1
options: Ratify Launch Definition v1 (5 gen1 categories on v2 + amendments A/B)
decisions.recommendation: Ratify (TASK-126). Establishes a fixed, testable launch finish line so P1-P5 cannot creep.
decisions.decision: Ratify Launch Definition v1. LAUNCH = Operational Readiness (5 gen1 categories - maadanim, hummus, snacks, yogurts, bread - LIVE on v2, score-frozen, field-complete; methodology page; >=1 flagship article; v2 Phase 1+2; confidence gate passing; validate-corpus green; fresh mobile+lg QA baselines; 0 P0/P1; dashboard GREEN) AND Market-Facing Readiness (comparison pages publicly available + indexable; first flagship article published; 5/5 category coverage; user-feedback collection active; SEO baseline live; analytics active). Amendment A: milk launches LEGACY-labelled (double migration gen0->gen1->v2 is disproportionate for a 20-product non-shelf editorial corpus) with a COMMITTED post-launch migration (P1 sub-task 128E), not optional. Amendment B: Launch Success Indicators (market-facing, section 6.5) added. NOT gating launch: Wave 2, base_pct, BSIP2 next-gen, all 3 articles, milk v2.
decisions.notes: Full definition: C:/Bari/launch_definition_v1.md. Roadmap: C:/Bari/bari_objective_roadmap_v1.md. P1-P5 success criteria inherit the launch dimension each objective owns (launch_definition_v1.md section 8).

decisions.title: BSIP2 TASK-133 revision calibration & scope sign-off (F2/F1/F4)
options: A - Approve recommended package: build the structure now and defer numeric magnitudes (F2 discount curve, F1 penalty deltas, F4 penalty size) to a calibration run against real corpora; F2 discounts the Protein Quality dimension only; treat proto_v0 as source-of-truth and re-sync the frozen spec at Phase E
B - Approve build but fix calibration magnitudes up front before coding
C - Changes requested to scope before build proceeds
decisions.recommendation: Option A. Three gates not derivable from architecture: (G1) calibration magnitudes - 47-81% DIAAS is a band not a per-product number; constants.py states calibration is a separate phase, so build structure now and set numbers in a calibration run. (G2) F2 scope - discount the protein-QUALITY contribution only, leaving protein mass feeding satiety/nutrient-density (recommended). (G3) spec reconciliation - prototype has drifted from frozen spec (code: protein_quality 0.10, additive 0.10, processing 0.15; methodology.md says 9/7/18%); recommend proto_v0 as source-of-truth with a Phase-E spec re-sync. Directly affects F2's effective impact via protein weight.
decisions.notes: Does NOT block starting 133A (taxonomy) or the structural build of B/C/D - only the calibrated magnitudes and final closure. Full plan: C:/Bari/research/TASK-133_implementation_roadmap.md. Origin: TASK-132 evaluation.

alerts.resolution_path: Close or unblock an active task (or move one to RETURNED).
Resolve the blocker, then set status to IN_PROGRESS.
Resolve the blocker, then set status to IN_PROGRESS.
Resolve the blocker, then set status to IN_PROGRESS.
